project.name: reflaxe.c / hxc
rules: Issue dependency direction is child needs blocker.
Children of an epic are parallel unless explicit blocking edges exist.
Create discovered work with a discovered-from relationship instead of expanding claimed scope silently.
Close an issue only after its acceptance commands and evidence pass.

epics.title: Repository and governance foundation
epics.description: Ratify contracts, make the scaffold build reproducibly, and establish the project rules Codex must follow.

epics.title: Reflaxe compiler core and C AST
epics.description: Build the deterministic AST/IR/output foundation before broad language lowering.

epics.title: Primitive semantics, control flow, and UB discipline
epics.description: Deliver the first correct executable Haxe-to-C vertical slice with explicit evaluation order and no C undefined behavior.

epics.title: Aggregates, generics, classes, and dispatch
epics.description: Lower Haxe's structured type system into readable C layouts and calls without defaulting to a universal box.

epics.title: Runtime, memory, dynamic values, and exceptions
epics.description: Implement selective runtime capabilities and full portable object-graph semantics while preserving a proven runtime-free lane.

epics.title: Haxe standard-library parity
epics.description: Systematically support the Haxe standard library with direct lowering, target-owned adapters, and narrow runtime slices.

epics.title: C interop and deterministic bindgen
epics.description: Make existing C libraries easy and safe to consume from exact headers, flags, and ABI facts.

epics.title: Exported C ABI and shared libraries
epics.description: Make Haxe-authored code a first-class producer of stable C headers, static libraries, and shared objects.

epics.title: CLI, build adapters, diagnostics, and inspection
epics.description: Turn compiler components into the coherent hxc product and an automation-friendly build surface.

epics.title: Examples, todo CLI, documentation, and adoption
epics.description: Prove the product through honest, polished examples and a macro-powered SQLite todo CLI.

epics.title: Quality, performance, security, and releases
epics.description: Continuously harden correctness, ABI, security, performance, compatibility, and release provenance.

tasks.title: Ratify product contracts and open ADR decisions
tasks.outcome: Resolve the owner decisions that affect public semantics before implementation hardens accidental choices.
acceptance: Record decisions for license, C baseline, string encoding, portable collector, exception ABI, and 1.0 platforms.
Update affected ADRs and PRD sections without contradictory language.
Create Beads discoveries for deferred experiments; do not hide undecided semantics in code.
tasks.description:
Resolve the owner decisions that affect public semantics before implementation hardens accidental choices.

PRD: §8, §9, §28

tasks.title: Choose license and complete third-party notices
tasks.outcome: Replace the scaffold license placeholder with an approved repository license and auditable notices.
acceptance: Canonical license text is checked in.
Reflaxe, Haxe-derived standard-library work, Clang tooling, and runtime dependencies have attribution/provenance entries.
CI rejects missing license/notice files in release artifacts.
tasks.description:
Replace the scaffold license placeholder with an approved repository license and auditable notices.

PRD: §22, §28

tasks.title: Pin Haxe, Reflaxe, and dependency resolution
tasks.outcome: Create a reproducible source-checkout and package build using the exact accepted Haxe 5 custom-target carrier and a known Reflaxe revision/version.
acceptance: Fresh checkout resolves dependencies without relying on the caller's current working directory.
CompilerBootstrap runs before CompilerInit and only for C builds.
The pin and update procedure are documented and CI-verifiable.
tasks.description:
Create a reproducible source-checkout and package build using Haxe 5.0.0-preview.1, its locked platform artifacts, and a known Reflaxe revision/version.

PRD: §2.2, §2.3, §25 M0

tasks.title: Make the bootstrap Haxe sources compile
tasks.outcome: Type-check the compiler adapter, CLI, C AST seed, macros, and c.* surface under the pinned toolchain.
acceptance: A dedicated HXML compiles all target-owned Haxe modules.
Macro and non-macro branches type-check where applicable.
Unsupported compiler execution ends with HXC1000 and cannot emit plausible program C.
tasks.description:
Type-check the compiler adapter, CLI, C AST seed, macros, and c.* surface under the pinned toolchain.

PRD: §10.3, §25 M0

tasks.title: Establish CI structure and runtime smoke matrix
tasks.outcome: Run structural, JSON, Haxe, C11 runtime, C++ header, and native fixture checks on every change.
acceptance: GCC and Clang compile runtime/fixtures with warnings as errors.
A C++ compiler consumes public C headers.
CI output identifies skipped optional tools and never reports an unrun required gate as passed.
tasks.description:
Run structural, JSON, Haxe, C11 runtime, C++ header, and native fixture checks on every change.

PRD: §21, §25 M0

tasks.title: Create stable diagnostic catalog and policy
tasks.outcome: Reserve diagnostic ranges and document wording, severity, source position, remediation, and stability rules.
acceptance: All current diagnostics appear in a machine-readable catalog.
Tests assert IDs rather than fragile full prose where appropriate.
Internal compiler failures are distinct from unsupported-source diagnostics.
tasks.description:
Reserve diagnostic ranges and document wording, severity, source position, remediation, and stability rules.

PRD: §20.1

tasks.title: Finalize contribution, security, and release governance
tasks.outcome: Give human and agent contributors a single operational contract for changes, disclosures, generated files, and releases.
acceptance: AGENTS and CONTRIBUTING agree on Beads workflow and definition of done.
SECURITY names a private reporting path and supported versions policy.
Generated files, vendoring, release signing, and provenance responsibilities are explicit.
tasks.description:
Give human and agent contributors a single operational contract for changes, disclosures, generated files, and releases.

PRD: §22, §29

tasks.title: Validate and materialize the Beads bootstrap graph
tasks.outcome: Prove the checked-in plan is acyclic, idempotently materializable, and useful to Codex.
acceptance: Dry-run works without bd installed.
Apply creates epics, children, labels, descriptions, acceptance criteria, and blocking edges.
A second apply creates no duplicate issues, including after issues have been closed.
bd ready exposes parallel starter work consistent with the graph.
tasks.description:
Prove the checked-in plan is acyclic, idempotently materializable, and useful to Codex.

PRD: §24

tasks.title: Define fixture taxonomy and snapshot update policy
tasks.outcome: Standardize positive, negative, AST, C snapshot, runtime, differential, ABI, and performance fixtures.
acceptance: Each fixture type has a directory, runner contract, and expected outputs.
Snapshot regeneration requires explicit command and shows semantic diffs.
Examples are not silently used as tests without declared assertions.
tasks.description:
Standardize positive, negative, AST, C snapshot, runtime, differential, ABI, and performance fixtures.

PRD: §21.1, §21.2, §21.3

tasks.title: Publish bootstrap capability and limitation manifest
tasks.outcome: Expose what the scaffold implements, reserves, rejects, and has not validated in machine-readable form.
acceptance: Manifest distinguishes implemented, scaffold-only, experimental, and unsupported capabilities.
README and hxc doctor derive or agree with the manifest.
No full-stdlib, production, or performance claim appears before its gate passes.
tasks.description:
Expose what the scaffold implements, reserves, rejects, and has not validated in machine-readable form.

PRD: §18.5, §25 M0

tasks.title: Complete the C11 AST declarator and type model
tasks.outcome: Represent all C declarator shapes structurally, including nested pointer/array/function declarations, qualifiers, attributes, and incomplete types.
acceptance: Golden tests cover precedence-sensitive declarations in parameters, typedefs, fields, and function pointers.
No normal compiler path stores user-derived C syntax as an opaque string.
AST can represent C11 constructs needed by M2-M7 without printer-specific hacks.
tasks.description:
Represent all C declarator shapes structurally, including nested pointer/array/function declarations, qualifiers, attributes, and incomplete types.

PRD: §10.6, §25 M1

tasks.title: Finish expression and statement printer precedence
tasks.outcome: Print readable C with the minimum safe parentheses and deterministic formatting.
acceptance: Every unary/binary/conditional/call/member/cast precedence pair has tests.
Printed fixtures compile under GCC and Clang strict C11.
Printer escaping handles identifiers, strings, characters, comments, and line directives safely.
tasks.description:
Print readable C with the minimum safe parentheses and deterministic formatting.

PRD: §10.6, §25 M1

tasks.title: Implement include and declaration dependency planning
tasks.outcome: Compute forward declarations, complete-type requirements, header/source placement, and deterministic include order.
acceptance: Pointer-only cycles use forward declarations.
By-value cycles produce an actionable diagnostic.
System/local includes are deduplicated and stable.
Headers are independently compilable.
tasks.description:
Compute forward declarations, complete-type requirements, header/source placement, and deterministic include order.

PRD: §10.10

tasks.title: Implement deterministic symbol registry and naming
tasks.outcome: Map Haxe modules/types/fields/locals/specializations to collision-free C identifiers with readable provenance.
acceptance: C keywords and implementation-reserved identifiers are escaped.
Overloads/generic specializations remain stable across filesystem paths and discovery order.
Collision diagnostics identify both source symbols.
Public @:c.name values are validated rather than rewritten silently.
tasks.description:
Map Haxe modules/types/fields/locals/specializations to collision-free C identifiers with readable provenance.

PRD: §10.8

tasks.title: Expand HxcIR semantic core
tasks.outcome: Represent explicit evaluation order, places/values, cleanup scopes, calls, dispatch, conversion, failure edges, and initialization independently of C syntax.
acceptance: IR invariants are documented and validated.
IR dump is deterministic and source-position aware.
Side effects and cleanup order can be tested before C emission.
Unsupported typed AST nodes become explicit diagnostics, not opaque values.
tasks.description:
Represent explicit evaluation order, places/values, cleanup scopes, calls, dispatch, conversion, failure edges, and initialization independently of C syntax.

PRD: §10.2, §10.5

tasks.title: Build typed-AST collection and normalization adapter
tasks.outcome: Collect all relevant Reflaxe module types into per-build normalized input while preserving source metadata and ownership.
acceptance: Compiler-server builds reset all mutable state.
Externs, abstracts, typedefs, metadata, entry point, and module ownership are classified.
Input order changes do not change normalized output.
A typed AST inventory report is available for unsupported-node planning.
tasks.description:
Collect all relevant Reflaxe module types into per-build normalized input while preserving source metadata and ownership.

PRD: §10.3, §10.4

tasks.title: Implement deterministic multi-file project emitter
tasks.outcome: Emit owned headers, sources, runtime-plan, compiler manifest, ABI placeholders, and build metadata through Reflaxe OutputManager.
acceptance: Generated-file ownership safely deletes stale files and never touches user-owned files.
Writes are content-addressed or skipped when unchanged.
Two builds in different absolute directories are byte-identical after declared path normalization.
Scaffold marker path remains fail-closed until real lowering replaces it.
tasks.description:
Emit owned headers, sources, runtime-plan, compiler manifest, ABI placeholders, and build metadata through Reflaxe OutputManager.

PRD: §10.9, §25 M1

tasks.title: Emit CMake, Meson, and raw manifest build seeds
tasks.outcome: Generate optional build-system adapters from one neutral compile/link plan.
acceptance: CMake and Meson consume the same source/include/define/library model.
Users can ignore generated adapters and read the JSON manifest.
Paths and flags are quoted safely.
Generated projects build the AST/printer fixtures.
tasks.description:
Generate optional build-system adapters from one neutral compile/link plan.

PRD: §18.3, §25 M1

tasks.title: Add determinism and stale-output adversarial tests
tasks.outcome: Exercise changed module order, absolute paths, removed modules, renamed symbols, line endings, and cached compiler-server runs.
acceptance: Byte-for-byte checks pass across isolated roots.
Removed output is deleted only when listed as compiler-owned.
No timestamp, random ID, map iteration, or host path leaks into normal artifacts.
Failure reports identify first differing artifact and offset.
tasks.description:
Exercise changed module order, absolute paths, removed modules, renamed symbols, line endings, and cached compiler-server runs.

PRD: §21.2, §25 M1

tasks.title: Create AST fixture compiler and round-trip corpus
tasks.outcome: Allow tests to construct C AST directly, emit translation units, and compile/link/run them without Haxe language lowering.
acceptance: Corpus covers declarations, precedence, initializers, control flow, attributes, and header/source splits.
Both GCC and Clang are supported.
Compiler invocations and outputs are recorded for reproducibility.
The harness is reusable by later lowering tasks.
tasks.description:
Allow tests to construct C AST directly, emit translation units, and compile/link/run them without Haxe language lowering.

PRD: §21.1

tasks.title: Map primitive Haxe types and conversions
tasks.outcome: Define exact C representations and conversion rules for Void, Bool, Int, Float, nullability, and target ABI integers.
acceptance: Representation table is an ADR-backed machine-readable contract.
Narrowing, widening, NaN, infinity, signed/unsigned, and nullable cases have tests.
Portable and metal mappings differ only where their source contracts require it.
No host-dependent `long` assumption leaks into ABI types.
tasks.description:
Define exact C representations and conversion rules for Void, Bool, Int, Float, nullability, and target ABI integers.

PRD: §12.1

tasks.title: Lower constants, locals, blocks, and return
tasks.outcome: Produce the first real HxcIR/C lowering for literal expressions and structured function bodies.
acceptance: Source positions survive into diagnostics and optional line mapping.
Local names are deterministic and shadowing-safe.
Void/value returns are validated.
Unsupported expressions fail at their exact source location.
tasks.description:
Produce the first real HxcIR/C lowering for literal expressions and structured function bodies.

PRD: §12.1, §12.14

tasks.title: Lower static functions, arguments, and direct calls
tasks.outcome: Emit prototypes/definitions and direct calls with explicit parameter/result representations.
acceptance: Entry-point signature is correct for executable artifacts.
Default/optional/rest arguments either lower correctly or produce scoped diagnostics.
Argument conversions and evaluation are represented in HxcIR.
Recursion and mutual recursion compile through header planning.
tasks.description:
Emit prototypes/definitions and direct calls with explicit parameter/result representations.

PRD: §12.10

tasks.title: Serialize Haxe evaluation order explicitly
tasks.outcome: Introduce temporaries and sequencing so generated behavior never depends on C's unspecified operand/argument order.
acceptance: Adversarial side-effect fixtures cover calls, assignments, fields, indexing, short circuit, ternary, and increments.
IR shows the chosen order explicitly.
Optimization passes may remove temporaries only with a proof/test.
Differential execution matches a reference Haxe target.
tasks.description:
Introduce temporaries and sequencing so generated behavior never depends on C's unspecified operand/argument order.

PRD: §12.2

tasks.title: Implement UB-safe integer and floating operations
tasks.outcome: Preserve Haxe arithmetic semantics without signed overflow, invalid shifts, division overflow, or unchecked conversion undefined behavior.
acceptance: INT_MIN/-1, divide-by-zero, negative shifts, overflow, modulo, and bit operations have boundary tests.
Generated code passes UBSan where supported.
Runtime helpers are used only where direct C cannot express the contract safely.
Fast paths remain inspectable under optimization.
tasks.description:
Preserve Haxe arithmetic semantics without signed overflow, invalid shifts, division overflow, or unchecked conversion undefined behavior.

PRD: §11.4, §12.1

tasks.title: Lower if, short-circuit, loops, switch, and jumps
tasks.outcome: Implement structured control flow while preserving expression values, break/continue targets, and cleanup obligations.
acceptance: Nested loops/switches and labeled internal jumps are tested.
Short-circuit operands execute exactly as Haxe specifies.
No user-observable fallthrough is introduced.
Unreachable or unsupported control flow has stable diagnostics.
tasks.description:
Implement structured control flow while preserving expression values, break/continue targets, and cleanup obligations.

PRD: §12.14

tasks.title: Lower string literals and minimal trace/Sys.println
tasks.outcome: Support immutable literals and the minimal hosted output path needed by hello without pulling the full object runtime.
acceptance: Literal bytes/length and chosen encoding match the string ADR.
NUL bytes are represented explicitly.
Sys.println handles errors according to the hosted policy.
Runtime plan records only required string/I/O slices.
tasks.description:
Support immutable literals and the minimal hosted output path needed by hello without pulling the full object runtime.

PRD: §12.3, §14.4

tasks.title: Lower fixed arrays and span-based iteration
tasks.outcome: Support compile-time fixed arrays and c.Span/c.ConstSpan without the general Haxe Array runtime.
acceptance: Bounds behavior is explicit by profile/build mode.
Pointer arithmetic is element-scaled and overflow-aware.
For loops over spans compile to ordinary indexed/pointer loops.
Metal no-runtime fixture links no hxrt symbol.
tasks.description:
Support compile-time fixed arrays and c.Span/c.ConstSpan without the general Haxe Array runtime.

PRD: §12.4, §17.1

tasks.title: Implement deterministic static initialization phase
tasks.outcome: Order module/class initialization explicitly and reject unresolvable cycles.
acceptance: Initialization graph and reasons are inspectable.
Each initializer runs once before first required use according to the selected strategy.
Cycles have a deterministic diagnostic path.
No compiler discovery order affects output or behavior.
tasks.description:
Order module/class initialization explicitly and reject unresolvable cycles.

PRD: §12.15

tasks.title: Complete and run the hello vertical slice
tasks.outcome: Compile examples/hello from Haxe through generated C to a native executable.
acceptance: Exact stdout is `Hello from hxc` plus one newline.
GCC and Clang builds are warning-clean.
Output is deterministic and readable against the documented baseline.
Runtime-plan contains a source reason for every selected feature and no broad object/GC/reflection slice.
tasks.description:
Compile examples/hello from Haxe through generated C to a native executable.

PRD: §19.1, §25 M2

tasks.title: Create primitive differential and sanitizer suite
tasks.outcome: Run generated C against a selected Haxe reference target over edge cases and randomized primitive programs.
acceptance: Seeded randomized cases are reproducible.
Semantic mismatches minimize to stored fixtures.
ASan/UBSan are clean on supported hosts.
Known intentional divergences require explicit ledger entries and tests.
tasks.description:
Run generated C against a selected Haxe reference target over edge cases and randomized primitive programs.

PRD: §21.5, §21.6

tasks.title: Lower anonymous structures and value structs
tasks.outcome: Represent structurally known records as named/deduplicated C structs with clear value/reference behavior.
acceptance: Field order/layout policy is deterministic.
Anonymous structural equality does not accidentally become C nominal incompatibility.
By-value copies and address-taking are explicit.
Metal @:c.layout(c.Layout.Struct) validation rejects unsupported layout combinations.
tasks.description:
Represent structurally known records as named/deduplicated C structs with clear value/reference behavior.

PRD: §12.5, §17.2

tasks.title: Lower Haxe enums as tagged unions
tasks.outcome: Emit readable discriminants and payload unions, including recursive and generic cases.
acceptance: Nullary constructors may optimize to enums when ABI-safe.
Payload access is tag-checked in safe/debug configurations.
Pattern matches are exhaustive and compile to structured switch code.
Recursive representation avoids illegal infinitely sized C types.
tasks.description:
Emit readable discriminants and payload unions, including recursive and generic cases.

PRD: §12.6

tasks.title: Implement deterministic generic monomorphization
tasks.outcome: Specialize reachable generic code/types by normalized type arguments and share equivalent instances.
acceptance: Specialization keys are stable and collision-free.
Recursive generic discovery terminates.
Code-size and specialization reports are emitted.
Unsupported dynamic/open generic cases have precise diagnostics or explicit runtime strategy.
tasks.description:
Specialize reachable generic code/types by normalized type arguments and share equivalent instances.

PRD: §12.9

tasks.title: Define and emit class instance layouts
tasks.outcome: Lower fields, inheritance prefix/layout, object headers, and null/reference representation according to profile/runtime needs.
acceptance: Layout has compile-time assertions where ABI relevant.
Base/derived conversions are safe and inspectable.
Portable metadata is selective; metal value/opaque representations remain available.
Private implementation layout never leaks into exported ABI by default.
tasks.description:
Lower fields, inheritance prefix/layout, object headers, and null/reference representation according to profile/runtime needs.

PRD: §12.7

tasks.title: Lower constructors, super calls, and field initialization
tasks.outcome: Preserve Haxe construction order, default field initialization, and failure cleanup.
acceptance: Super/field/body ordering matches Haxe semantics.
Partially initialized objects are cleaned safely on failure.
Empty/trivial constructors optimize away where proven.
Constructor cycles and unsupported native layouts diagnose clearly.
tasks.description:
Preserve Haxe construction order, default field initialization, and failure cleanup.

PRD: §12.7, §12.15

tasks.title: Implement virtual method dispatch and override validation
tasks.outcome: Emit minimal vtables only for reachable polymorphic calls and preserve override signatures.
acceptance: Nonvirtual/final/direct calls remain direct.
Vtable slots are deterministic across modules.
Override variance/representation mismatches are rejected.
Dispatch reports explain each indirect call.
tasks.description:
Emit minimal vtables only for reachable polymorphic calls and preserve override signatures.

PRD: §12.7

tasks.title: Implement interface tables and casts
tasks.outcome: Represent reachable interfaces with compact itables or equivalent descriptors and safe cast checks.
acceptance: Multiple interfaces and inherited interfaces work.
Failed casts follow Haxe semantics.
Unused interfaces emit no tables.
Interface method calls remain ABI-internal unless explicitly exported.
tasks.description:
Represent reachable interfaces with compact itables or equivalent descriptors and safe cast checks.

PRD: §12.8

tasks.title: Lower function values and closures
tasks.outcome: Represent function values as typed function/context pairs and allocate environments only when required.
acceptance: Nonescaping captures can live on stack/region.
Escaping closures retain captured values safely.
Recursive closures work.
Calling convention and cleanup are represented explicitly.
tasks.description:
Represent function values as typed function/context pairs and allocate environments only when required.

PRD: §12.10

tasks.title: Add devirtualization and escape-analysis passes
tasks.outcome: Prove and apply direct-call, stack-allocation, scalar-replacement, and dead-metadata optimizations without changing semantics.
acceptance: Every optimization has a reportable proof/reason and opt-out debug mode.
Differential suite runs with passes on/off.
Nonescaping closure/object benchmark performs zero heap allocations.
Optimization never changes public ABI artifacts.
tasks.description:
Prove and apply direct-call, stack-allocation, scalar-replacement, and dead-metadata optimizations without changing semantics.

PRD: §13.4, §21.7

tasks.title: Build aggregate/generic/class semantic corpus
tasks.outcome: Create positive, negative, recursive, cross-module, and differential fixtures for M3 semantics.
acceptance: Recursive enums, generic containers, inheritance, interfaces, closures, and anonymous records are covered.
GCC/Clang warning and sanitizer gates pass.
Generated C snapshots are reviewed for readability.
M3 capability manifest is updated from evidence.
tasks.description:
Create positive, negative, recursive, cross-module, and differential fixtures for M3 semantics.

PRD: §21

tasks.title: Implement runtime feature graph and selective packaging
tasks.outcome: Turn runtime feature IDs/dependencies/reasons into deterministic source/header/link selection.
acceptance: Feature closure is deterministic and cycle-checked.
Every selected feature has at least one source reason.
Unselected runtime files/symbols are absent from build plan.
Manual overrides are validated against profile/runtime policy.
tasks.description:
Turn runtime feature IDs/dependencies/reasons into deterministic source/header/link selection.

PRD: §13.1

tasks.title: Harden allocator ABI and custom allocator integration
tasks.outcome: Finalize aligned allocation/reallocation/release contracts for hosted, freestanding, and exported ownership.
acceptance: Overflow/alignment/zero-size behavior is specified and tested.
Custom allocator fixture works without libc allocation.
Allocator identity follows allocations that cross boundaries.
C and C++ ABI/layout tests pass on supported platforms.
tasks.description:
Finalize aligned allocation/reallocation/release contracts for hosted, freestanding, and exported ownership.

PRD: §13.2

tasks.title: Implement string runtime and encoding contract
tasks.outcome: Provide immutable/mutable string primitives, UTF policy, hashing, comparison, slicing, conversion, and C boundary views.
acceptance: Embedded NUL and invalid encoding policy are tested.
Borrowed CString lifetime and owned conversion are explicit.
Common literal/concat/compare paths are direct and allocation-aware.
String feature can link without object/GC/reflection when possible.
tasks.description:
Provide immutable/mutable string primitives, UTF policy, hashing, comparison, slicing, conversion, and C boundary views.

PRD: §12.3, §13, ADR 0004

tasks.title: Implement Haxe Array runtime slice
tasks.outcome: Support resizable typed arrays with correct length/capacity, bounds, mutation, iteration, and element retention.
acceptance: Primitive and reference element arrays are covered.
Growth overflow and allocation failure are safe.
Array operations preserve Haxe semantics under aliasing.
Metal fixed arrays/spans do not depend on this slice.
tasks.description:
Support resizable typed arrays with correct length/capacity, bounds, mutation, iteration, and element retention.

PRD: §12.4

tasks.title: Implement object/type descriptor core
tasks.outcome: Define minimal portable object headers/descriptors needed by collection, dispatch, reflection, and cleanup.
acceptance: Descriptors are immutable/deterministic and only emitted when reachable.
Object size/alignment/trace/finalization information is validated.
Metal no-runtime types have no hidden header.
Descriptor ABI is internal and versioned.
tasks.description:
Define minimal portable object headers/descriptors needed by collection, dispatch, reflection, and cleanup.

PRD: §13.1, §12.12

tasks.title: Implement selected portable collector
tasks.outcome: Implement the ratified collector with precise roots/fields, cycles, allocation pressure, and deterministic integration points.
acceptance: Cyclic graphs are reclaimed.
Interior/foreign pointers follow documented restrictions.
Stack/global/thread roots are correct on supported platforms.
Collector stress suite is sanitizer-clean and reports pauses/allocations.
tasks.description:
Implement the ratified collector with precise roots/fields, cycles, allocation pressure, and deterministic integration points.

PRD: §13.3, ADR 0005

tasks.title: Implement dynamic value representation and operations
tasks.outcome: Box only source sites that require Dynamic/heterogeneous representation and implement conversions, fields, calls, equality, and errors.
acceptance: Typed code paths do not route through Dynamic.
Tag/payload representation is UB-safe.
Boxing reasons appear in inspection reports.
Differential fixtures cover primitives, objects, enums, functions, and null.
tasks.description:
Box only source sites that require Dynamic/heterogeneous representation and implement conversions, fields, calls, equality, and errors.

PRD: §12.11

tasks.title: Implement selective reflection and RTTI slices
tasks.outcome: Support Type/Reflect/runtime type information only for operations and types proven reachable.
acceptance: Feature-level and type-level reflection reachability are inspectable.
Metadata not used by the program is absent.
Field lookup/call/create semantics match Haxe for supported cases.
No-runtime builds receive reasoned errors for reflective operations.
tasks.description:
Support Type/Reflect/runtime type information only for operations and types proven reachable.

PRD: §12.12

tasks.title: Implement exceptions, catch, finally, and cleanup regions
tasks.outcome: Preserve throw/catch/finally behavior with explicit cleanup and no exception crossing a public C ABI.
acceptance: Nested try/catch/finally and rethrow order matches Haxe.
Resources and partially initialized values clean up exactly once.
Result lowering is used where statically/legal and reported.
General runtime exception mechanism is portable across supported compilers or scoped by platform.
tasks.description:
Preserve throw/catch/finally behavior with explicit cleanup and no exception crossing a public C ABI.

PRD: §12.13, §13.6

tasks.title: Implement no-runtime eligibility proof and diagnostics
tasks.outcome: Decide whether a whole program can honor hxc_runtime=none and explain every blocker with source provenance.
acceptance: Eligible primitive/span fixtures link with no hxrt symbols or files.
Ineligible programs fail before native link with sorted blockers.
No helper is selected merely because a type was seen but not used.
Proof is emitted in runtime-plan JSON.
tasks.description:
Decide whether a whole program can honor hxc_runtime=none and explain every blocker with source provenance.

PRD: §9.4, §13.5

tasks.title: Version runtime ABI and feature manifests
tasks.outcome: Define internal/public runtime ABI compatibility, feature schema versions, and generated-code checks.
acceptance: Generated code rejects incompatible runtime major versions.
Feature manifests are machine-diffable.
Public exported APIs do not accidentally expose unstable runtime structs.
Release artifacts include runtime source/build provenance.
tasks.description:
Define internal/public runtime ABI compatibility, feature schema versions, and generated-code checks.

PRD: §13.1, §16.8

tasks.title: Create runtime stress, failure, and sanitizer suite
tasks.outcome: Exercise allocation failure, cycles, deep graphs, strings/arrays, exception cleanup, threads where applicable, and corrupted inputs.
acceptance: ASan/UBSan/LSan and platform equivalents are clean.
Deterministic fault injection reaches every allocation site class.
Stress seeds and limits are reproducible.
Runtime test failures identify selected feature/version/configuration.
tasks.description:
Exercise allocation failure, cycles, deep graphs, strings/arrays, exception cleanup, threads where applicable, and corrupted inputs.

PRD: §21.6

tasks.title: Generate and maintain the stdlib ownership/parity ledger
tasks.outcome: Inventory Haxe std modules/APIs and assign direct lowering, target override, runtime helper, native adapter, unsupported, and test provenance.
acceptance: Ledger is generated/checkable against the pinned Haxe std tree.
Every unsupported/partial API has a linked Beads issue and diagnostic policy.
Rows include profile/environment/runtime/test ownership.
CI prevents unreviewed ledger drift on Haxe upgrades.
tasks.description:
Inventory Haxe std modules/APIs and assign direct lowering, target override, runtime helper, native adapter, unsupported, and test provenance.

PRD: §14.2, §14.3

tasks.title: Implement String and Unicode stdlib parity
tasks.outcome: Support String, StringBuf, Unicode/code-point operations, and encoding conversions to the agreed compatibility level.
acceptance: Pinned upstream tests for the scope pass or have explicit ledger gaps.
ASCII fast paths and Unicode correctness are both tested.
Invalid sequences and indexing semantics are documented.
C boundary conversions expose ownership and failure.
tasks.description:
Support String, StringBuf, Unicode/code-point operations, and encoding conversions to the agreed compatibility level.

PRD: §14.4

tasks.title: Implement core collection parity
tasks.outcome: Support Array, Vector, List, StringMap, IntMap, ObjectMap, EnumValueMap, and iterators with typed representations.
acceptance: Collection semantics and mutation during iteration match Haxe where specified.
Generic specialization avoids universal boxing when types are known.
Hash/equality behavior is explicit for dynamic/object keys.
Upstream and adversarial tests pass.
tasks.description:
Support Array, Vector, List, StringMap, IntMap, ObjectMap, EnumValueMap, and iterators with typed representations.

PRD: §14.4

tasks.title: Implement Bytes, Buffer, and core I/O parity
tasks.outcome: Support haxe.io.Bytes/BytesBuffer/Input/Output and endian/encoding operations with safe bounds and partial I/O.
acceptance: Binary round-trips and boundary failures match Haxe.
Large lengths cannot overflow native allocation or pointer arithmetic.
Hosted and custom stream adapters are separable.
No hidden text conversion occurs in byte APIs.
tasks.description:
Support haxe.io.Bytes/BytesBuffer/Input/Output and endian/encoding operations with safe bounds and partial I/O.

PRD: §14.4

tasks.title: Implement Std, Math, Type, and Reflect parity
tasks.outcome: Cover core conversion, math, type inspection/creation, and reflection surfaces with selective runtime use.
acceptance: Math edge cases follow Haxe target contract.
Std.string/parse/casts have differential tests.
Type/Reflect only retain necessary descriptors.
No-runtime diagnostics identify reflective dependency chains.
tasks.description:
Cover core conversion, math, type inspection/creation, and reflection surfaces with selective runtime use.

PRD: §14

tasks.title: Implement JSON and serialization support
tasks.outcome: Support haxe.Json parse/stringify and required serialization primitives with bounded resource behavior.
acceptance: Round-trip and malformed-input corpus passes.
Depth/size limits and error positions are explicit.
Typed fast paths do not require reflection where generated serializers suffice.
Fuzzing hooks are available.
tasks.description:
Support haxe.Json parse/stringify and required serialization primitives with bounded resource behavior.

PRD: §14.4

tasks.title: Implement regular-expression support
tasks.outcome: Choose/integrate a regex backend or scoped implementation that meets Haxe EReg semantics on supported platforms.
acceptance: Backend/license/feature mapping is documented.
EReg matching, groups, replace, split, and global state tests pass.
Unsupported syntax is detected deterministically.
Runtime feature and native dependency are explicit.
tasks.description:
Choose/integrate a regex backend or scoped implementation that meets Haxe EReg semantics on supported platforms.

PRD: §14.4

tasks.title: Implement Date and time adapters
tasks.outcome: Support Date/timezone/timestamp operations through portable and platform adapters.
acceptance: UTC/local/DST boundary corpus is deterministic with controlled timezone data.
Precision/range conversions are overflow-safe.
Freestanding/WASI limitations are explicit.
Clock APIs distinguish wall and monotonic time.
tasks.description:
Support Date/timezone/timestamp operations through portable and platform adapters.

PRD: §14.4

tasks.title: Implement filesystem, environment, and process adapters
tasks.outcome: Support sys.FileSystem, sys.io.File, environment, command/process, paths, and errors on hosted targets.
acceptance: POSIX and Windows path/error behavior has platform tests.
Argument/environment escaping does not invoke a shell unless requested.
WASI/freestanding capability errors are explicit.
Resources close on success and failure.
tasks.description:
Support sys.FileSystem, sys.io.File, environment, command/process, paths, and errors on hosted targets.

PRD: §14.4, §12.18

tasks.title: Implement socket/network adapters
tasks.outcome: Support the scoped sys.net surface with portable address/error/blocking semantics.
acceptance: TCP/UDP/local resolution fixtures run on supported CI.
Timeout/nonblocking behavior is explicit.
Windows socket initialization/cleanup is correct.
No network tests rely on external internet services.
tasks.description:
Support the scoped sys.net surface with portable address/error/blocking semantics.

PRD: §14.4

tasks.title: Implement threads, synchronization, TLS, and atomics
tasks.outcome: Map Haxe thread primitives to C11/platform threads with collector/root and memory-model integration.
acceptance: Mutex/condition/semaphore/thread-local/atomic semantics are tested.
Collector sees thread roots safely.
Data-race tests use TSan where available.
Single-thread/freestanding builds do not link thread support.
tasks.description:
Map Haxe thread primitives to C11/platform threads with collector/root and memory-model integration.

PRD: §12.17, §14.4

tasks.title: Integrate upstream Haxe std tests and parity dashboard
tasks.outcome: Run the pinned upstream suite with explicit selection, results, gaps, and differential comparisons.
acceptance: No silent skip is possible.
Dashboard reports pass/fail/unsupported by module, profile, environment, and platform.
Every failure links to a Beads issue or approved divergence.
Release gates consume archived machine-readable results.
tasks.description:
Run the pinned upstream suite with explicit selection, results, gaps, and differential comparisons.

PRD: §14.5, §21.5

tasks.title: Design and implement Clang-backed bindgen driver
tasks.outcome: Invoke Clang with exact target/defines/includes/flags and capture a versioned semantic declaration model.
acceptance: Invocation is reproducible and inspectable.
Header parse diagnostics preserve source files/lines.
Clang version/target/flags enter the binding lock.
No regex parser is authoritative for ABI facts.
tasks.description:
Invoke Clang with exact target/defines/includes/flags and capture a versioned semantic declaration model.

PRD: §15.1, §15.3

tasks.title: Model preprocessor configuration and header entry sets
tasks.outcome: Represent translation-unit inputs, defines, include ordering, target triple, language mode, sysroot, and conditional declarations.
acceptance: Equivalent invocations normalize to stable configuration hashes.
Conflicting defines/includes are diagnosed.
Generated wrappers include only declarations reachable from configured roots.
Configuration provenance appears in generated comments/lock.
tasks.description:
Represent translation-unit inputs, defines, include ordering, target triple, language mode, sysroot, and conditional declarations.

PRD: §15.2, §15.3

tasks.title: Map C scalars, enums, typedefs, qualifiers, and constants
tasks.outcome: Generate exact raw Haxe extern representations for primitive ABI declarations.
acceptance: Integer width/signedness follows target ABI, not spelling assumptions.
Anonymous/named enums and macro constants have deterministic names.
Const/volatile/restrict and typedef identity are preserved where meaningful.
Compiled probes validate size/range/value mappings.
tasks.description:
Generate exact raw Haxe extern representations for primitive ABI declarations.

PRD: §15.4, §15.5

tasks.title: Map structs, unions, anonymous members, bitfields, and packing
tasks.outcome: Represent complete/incomplete aggregates and verify layout through Clang facts and generated C assertions/probes.
acceptance: Size/alignment/offset/bitfield fixtures pass across supported targets.
Flexible arrays and packed/aligned declarations are explicit.
Unsupported nonportable layout produces a precise diagnostic.
Opaque handles remain opaque by default.
tasks.description:
Represent complete/incomplete aggregates and verify layout through Clang facts and generated C assertions/probes.

PRD: §15.4

tasks.title: Map functions, function pointers, callbacks, and variadics
tasks.outcome: Generate callable externs with correct calling convention, nullability hooks, callback lifetime, and scoped variadic support.
acceptance: cdecl/stdcall/platform conventions are tested where available.
Callback trampolines retain/release environments safely.
Variadics require configured typed wrappers unless a safe direct form exists.
errno/out-parameter/error conventions remain available to wrapper mapping.
tasks.description:
Generate callable externs with correct calling convention, nullability hooks, callback lifetime, and scoped variadic support.

PRD: §15.4

tasks.title: Emit deterministic raw extern modules
tasks.outcome: Write minimally transformed, mechanically reviewable Haxe externs plus native build metadata.
acceptance: Regeneration from identical input is byte-identical.
User-authored wrappers live separately and are never overwritten.
Names have stable collision handling and source comments.
Generated extern package type-checks under Haxe and the target.
tasks.description:
Write minimally transformed, mechanically reviewable Haxe externs plus native build metadata.

PRD: §15.5

tasks.title: Implement declarative safe-wrapper mapping
tasks.outcome: Generate optional idiomatic wrappers from explicit ownership, nullability, length, error, string, handle, and lifetime mappings.
acceptance: Mapping file is schema-validated and versioned.
Unsafe raw layer remains accessible but clearly separated.
Ownership transfer and destructor selection are checked.
Wrapper tests include allocation failure and null/error paths.
tasks.description:
Generate optional idiomatic wrappers from explicit ownership, nullability, length, error, string, handle, and lifetime mappings.

PRD: §15.6

tasks.title: Generate binding locks, ABI probes, and drift reports
tasks.outcome: Record inputs and ABI facts so dependency/header changes are detected before runtime corruption.
acceptance: Lock includes header digests, flags, target, Clang version, declaration/layout hashes, and mappings.
Compiled probes compare size/alignment/offset/calling assumptions.
Drift report categorizes source-only, wrapper, and ABI-breaking changes.
CI can require explicit lock update.
tasks.description:
Record inputs and ABI facts so dependency/header changes are detected before runtime corruption.

PRD: §15.5, §16.8

tasks.title: Support pkg-config and compile_commands inputs
tasks.outcome: Resolve exact include/define/link context from common native build metadata without hiding the final command.
acceptance: pkg-config static/dynamic modes are distinguished.
compile_commands selection is deterministic for multiple entries.
Resolved flags are normalized and inspectable.
Environment-dependent paths are recorded with reproducibility warnings.
tasks.description:
Resolve exact include/define/link context from common native build metadata without hiding the final command.

PRD: §15.2

tasks.title: Generate supported C++ extern-C shims
tasks.outcome: Wrap configured C++ constructors/methods/destructors into opaque C handles and exception-safe C functions.
acceptance: No C++ class/template/STL/exceptions appear in generated public C header.
All C++ exceptions are caught and mapped to configured errors.
Ownership and destructor paths are tested.
Unsupported overload/template/lifetime cases are diagnosed rather than guessed.
tasks.description:
Wrap configured C++ constructors/methods/destructors into opaque C handles and exception-safe C functions.

PRD: §15.8, ADR 0003

tasks.title: Add advisory AI mapping workflow without ABI authority
tasks.outcome: Allow an LLM to propose names/docs/ownership mappings as a reviewable patch while deterministic parsed facts remain authoritative.
acceptance: AI is off by default and never runs during reproducible build/regeneration.
Prompt/context/output provenance is recorded when used.
Suggestions cannot alter sizes, calling conventions, layouts, or declarations without deterministic validation.
Human review diff clearly separates suggestions from facts.
tasks.description:
Allow an LLM to propose names/docs/ownership mappings as a reviewable patch while deterministic parsed facts remain authoritative.

PRD: §15.7

tasks.title: Prove bindgen with SQLite and zlib-class fixtures
tasks.outcome: Regenerate and use representative stable C libraries covering opaque handles, callbacks, buffers, errors, enums, and build metadata.
acceptance: Clean regeneration produces no diff.
Haxe examples compile/link/run against system or pinned fixture libraries.
Layout/ABI probes pass.
Unsupported platform differences are explicit in locks and CI matrix.
tasks.description:
Regenerate and use representative stable C libraries covering opaque handles, callbacks, buffers, errors, enums, and build metadata.

PRD: §15, §25 M6

tasks.title: Discover and validate exported declarations
tasks.outcome: Collect @:c.export and related public ABI metadata and build a deterministic export model independent of internal symbol names.
acceptance: Duplicate/invalid names and visibility conflicts are source-positioned.
Only explicitly exported declarations enter public ABI.
Generic/instance exports require concrete wrapper shape.
Export inventory is inspectable before C generation.
tasks.description:
Collect @:c.export and related public ABI metadata and build a deterministic export model independent of internal symbol names.

PRD: §16.1

tasks.title: Implement ABI-safe public type checker
tasks.outcome: Allow fixed scalars, declared value structs/enums, pointers/spans, callbacks, opaque handles, and explicit result/error shapes while rejecting internal Haxe/runtime layouts.
acceptance: Every public pointer has ownership and nullability.
By-value structs have stable layout assertions.
Strings/arrays/classes require explicit boundary mapping.
Diagnostics suggest a safe wrapper shape.
tasks.description:
Allow fixed scalars, declared value structs/enums, pointers/spans, callbacks, opaque handles, and explicit result/error shapes while rejecting internal Haxe/runtime layouts.

PRD: §16.2

tasks.title: Emit public C headers and implementation wrappers
tasks.outcome: Generate standalone, namespaced, documented C headers and wrappers that translate to internal calling conventions.
acceptance: Header compiles as C11 and C++17 with extern-C guards.
Include guards, visibility, calling conventions, and integer types are portable.
No private/internal runtime symbol leaks.
C and C++ consumers need only installed public artifacts.
tasks.description:
Generate standalone, namespaced, documented C headers and wrappers that translate to internal calling conventions.

PRD: §16.3, §16.4

tasks.title: Implement ownership, nullability, and error boundary adapters
tasks.outcome: Translate Haxe values/failures into explicit C contracts and generated destroy/free APIs.
acceptance: No Haxe exception crosses the C ABI.
Owned outputs have a matching release function/allocator contract.
Borrowed lifetimes are documented and testable.
Out-parameter/status/result conventions are configurable but deterministic.
tasks.description:
Translate Haxe values/failures into explicit C contracts and generated destroy/free APIs.

PRD: §16.5, §16.6

tasks.title: Emit visibility, symbol version, and export-map files
tasks.outcome: Hide internal symbols and export only the public ABI on ELF, Mach-O, and PE/COFF targets.
acceptance: nm/dumpbin-equivalent tests compare actual exports to manifest.
Version scripts/export lists/.def files are deterministic.
Static and shared builds use consistent public names.
Unsupported toolchain behavior is detected by doctor/build.
tasks.description:
Hide internal symbols and export only the public ABI on ELF, Mach-O, and PE/COFF targets.

PRD: §16.7

tasks.title: Build static and shared library artifacts
tasks.outcome: Produce archive/shared artifacts, import libraries where needed, and installation metadata from the neutral build plan.
acceptance: Linux .so, macOS .dylib, and Windows .dll paths are covered according to the supported matrix.
PIC/import/export flags are correct.
CMake/Meson/pkg-config installation artifacts work.
Reproducible build metadata records toolchain and inputs.
tasks.description:
Produce archive/shared artifacts, import libraries where needed, and installation metadata from the neutral build plan.

PRD: §16.7

tasks.title: Generate ABI manifest, hash, and compatibility diff
tasks.outcome: Describe public symbols/types/layout/ownership/calling convention/version in a stable schema and classify changes.
acceptance: Manifest hash is independent of documentation/formatting-only changes.
Diff detects removed/changed symbols, layout, enum values, ownership, nullability, and calling convention.
Intentional breaks require version policy action.
CI stores before/after reports.
tasks.description:
Describe public symbols/types/layout/ownership/calling convention/version in a stable schema and classify changes.

PRD: §16.8

tasks.title: Run independent C and C++ consumer matrix
tasks.outcome: Build/install/consume exported libraries outside the generator tree using multiple C/C++ compilers.
acceptance: Consumers include only public headers and link installed artifacts.
C++ sees no name mangling issue.
Owned/error paths and repeated load/unload are tested.
Consumer failures cannot pass due to in-tree private include paths.
tasks.description:
Build/install/consume exported libraries outside the generator tree using multiple C/C++ compilers.

PRD: §16.3, §25 M7

tasks.title: Generate optional header-only C++ RAII wrappers
tasks.outcome: Provide opt-in C++ convenience types over the stable C ABI without making C++ ABI part of the product contract.
acceptance: Wrapper is header-only or otherwise explicitly versioned.
Move/copy/destruction semantics match C ownership.
Exceptions are opt-in and never cross C functions.
Raw C API remains complete and primary.
tasks.description:
Provide opt-in C++ convenience types over the stable C ABI without making C++ ABI part of the product contract.

PRD: §16.4

tasks.title: Add foreign-language ABI smoke consumers
tasks.outcome: Demonstrate that ordinary C ABI artifacts load from at least Python ctypes and one additional FFI ecosystem without language-specific generator magic.
acceptance: Consumers run against installed shared library.
String/buffer/handle/error ownership is exercised.
Examples document platform loader paths safely.

tasks.description:

PRD: §16, §19

tasks.title: Implement hxc.json parser, schema validation, and precedence
tasks.outcome: Load strict configuration, overlays, CLI overrides, and direct defines into one provenance-aware build model.
acceptance: Unknown keys and invalid combinations fail before compilation.
Path resolution is independent of current working directory.
Effective config and source of every value are inspectable.
Schema and parser tests stay synchronized.

Load strict configuration, overlays, CLI overrides, and direct defines into one provenance-aware build model.

acceptance: Commands share error/diagnostic formatting.

tasks.title: Implement hxc new project templates

acceptance: Template output validates against schema and type-checks.

PRD: §18.1, §25 M8

tasks.title: Create neutral native build plan and toolchain adapters
tasks.outcome: Translate generated sources, runtime slices, native sources, includes, defines, libraries, artifact type, and target into exact compile/link steps.
acceptance: Plan is serializable and inspectable.
GCC/Clang/MSVC-family adapters quote arguments without shell concatenation.
Cross-compile/sysroot/target settings are explicit.

tasks.title: Implement build, run, test, and clean commands

acceptance: build can stop after codegen/objects/library/executable.

tasks.title: Complete hxc doctor capability diagnostics

acceptance: Human and JSON modes are stable.

PRD: §18.4

tasks.title: Implement hxc inspect reports
tasks.outcome: Expose config, typed inventory, HxcIR, C AST, lowering decisions, runtime plan, symbols, includes, build commands, macros, ABI, and size/allocation summaries.
acceptance: Reports have human and versioned JSON forms.

tasks.description:
Expose config, typed inventory, HxcIR, C AST, lowering decisions, runtime plan, symbols, includes, build commands, macros, ABI, and size/allocation summaries.

tasks.title: Implement source maps and native diagnostic remapping

acceptance: #line mode and sidecar map mode are supported/compared.

Matrix metadata is archived with releases.
Unsupported combinations fail during planning with clear reason.
tasks.description:
Run supported Haxe/C/C++ compiler versions, operating systems, architectures, profiles, runtime policies, artifacts, and environments with explicit tiering.

PRD: §21, §25 M10

tasks.title: Fuzz C AST printer and generated translation units
tasks.outcome: Generate/minimize valid ASTs and adversarial literals/declarators, compile them with multiple compilers, and detect crashes/misprints/non-determinism.
acceptance: Seed corpus and fuzz dictionaries are checked in.
Crashes/minimized cases become regression fixtures.
Resource limits prevent CI denial of service.
Printer never emits unterminated comments/strings/directives from source data.
tasks.description:
Generate/minimize valid ASTs and adversarial literals/declarators, compile them with multiple compilers, and detect crashes/misprints/non-determinism.

PRD: §21.6, §22

tasks.title: Fuzz bindgen inputs and mapping parser
tasks.outcome: Exercise malformed/complex headers, preprocessor configurations, mapping files, and lock/probe parsers without trusting unbounded input.
acceptance: No crash, uncontrolled resource use, path escape, or command injection.
Minimized findings become fixtures.
Clang subprocess time/memory/output limits are enforced.
Generated identifiers/comments cannot inject C/Haxe syntax.
tasks.description:
Exercise malformed/complex headers, preprocessor configurations, mapping files, and lock/probe parsers without trusting unbounded input.

PRD: §15, §22

tasks.title: Run sanitizer, leak, race, and static-analysis gates
tasks.outcome: Continuously analyze compiler helpers, runtime, generated programs, and native adapters with appropriate dynamic/static tools.
acceptance: ASan/UBSan/LSan are required on supported Unix CI; TSan runs scoped thread suites.
Static analyzer warnings are triaged, not blanket-suppressed.
Suppression files are minimal, documented, and reviewed.
Release evidence records tool versions and scope.
tasks.description:
Continuously analyze compiler helpers, runtime, generated programs, and native adapters with appropriate dynamic/static tools.

PRD: §21.6

tasks.title: Define and enforce performance/size budgets
tasks.outcome: Track compiler time/memory, generated C compile time, executable/library size, allocations, throughput, and startup with stable baselines.
acceptance: Benchmarks distinguish noise from regression and record toolchain/hardware.
Budgets exist for hello, kernels, runtime slices, bindgen, export, and todo.
Portable/runtime costs are attributed by feature.
Regressions require explicit approval or blocking issue.
tasks.description:
Track compiler time/memory, generated C compile time, executable/library size, allocations, throughput, and startup with stable baselines.

PRD: §21.7, §26

tasks.title: Perform compiler/runtime/interop security review
tasks.outcome: Threat-model generated-code injection, malicious headers/configs, path traversal, tool invocation, allocator/lifetime errors, shared-library boundaries, and supply chain.
acceptance: Threat model and mitigations are documented.
Security-sensitive code has targeted tests/review owners.
No shell command construction from untrusted arguments.
Disclosure/release response process is exercised.
tasks.description:
Threat-model generated-code injection, malicious headers/configs, path traversal, tool invocation, allocator/lifetime errors, shared-library boundaries, and supply chain.

PRD: §22

tasks.title: Publish compatibility and support matrix
tasks.outcome: Generate evidence-backed support status for Haxe features, stdlib modules, platforms, compilers, ABIs, environments, profiles, and artifacts.
acceptance: Statuses link to tests/results and known issues.
Experimental/partial/unsupported are distinct.
README claims are generated from or reviewed against matrix.
Matrix is versioned per release.
tasks.description:
Generate evidence-backed support status for Haxe features, stdlib modules, platforms, compilers, ABIs, environments, profiles, and artifacts.

PRD: §25 M10, §26

tasks.title: Automate signed, reproducible release artifacts
tasks.outcome: Build immutable source/package artifacts with checksums, signatures, SBOM, provenance, release notes, and rollback instructions.
acceptance: Release runs only from tagged clean commit.
Artifacts rebuild within documented reproducibility constraints.
Dependency/toolchain provenance is attached.
Install smoke tests run against downloaded artifacts.
tasks.description:
Build immutable source/package artifacts with checksums, signatures, SBOM, provenance, release notes, and rollback instructions.

PRD: §22, §25 M10

tasks.title: Ratify stable public ABI and compatibility policy
tasks.outcome: Define semantic versioning and compatibility guarantees for generated public C ABI, runtime ABI, manifests, bindings, CLI JSON, and config schema.
acceptance: Breaking/nonbreaking classifications have examples/tests.
ABI diff gate enforces version changes.
Unstable internal layouts are clearly excluded.
Deprecation and migration windows are documented.
tasks.description:
Define semantic versioning and compatibility guarantees for generated public C ABI, runtime ABI, manifests, bindings, CLI JSON, and config schema.

PRD: §16.8, §25 M10

tasks.title: Meet the 1.0 stdlib and semantic parity gate
tasks.outcome: Reach the ratified upstream test threshold with every remaining divergence explicit and release-approved.
acceptance: No silent skips or unknown failures.
Tier-1 semantic/stdlib dashboard meets threshold.
Known divergences have user-visible diagnostics/docs and post-1.0 issues.
Portable source suite includes representative real applications.
tasks.description:
Reach the ratified upstream test threshold with every remaining divergence explicit and release-approved.

PRD: §5 G2, §5 G7, §25 M10

tasks.title: Cut evidence-backed beta release
tasks.outcome: Ship a beta only after install/onboarding/examples/compatibility/security/ABI evidence is archived.
acceptance: Fresh-user smoke path succeeds on tier-1 platforms.
Known limitations and support matrix are prominent.
Feedback/crash/diagnostic collection path is documented.
No 1.0 stability promise is implied.
tasks.description:
Ship a beta only after install/onboarding/examples/compatibility/security/ABI evidence is archived.

PRD: §25 M10

tasks.title: Cut 1.0 and archive release evidence
tasks.outcome: Release 1.0 only after correctness, UB, security, ABI, performance, docs, examples, and support gates are satisfied.
acceptance: All release blockers are closed with linked evidence.
Signed immutable artifacts and provenance are published.
Post-1.0 compatibility/maintenance policy is active.
Release report records exact source, toolchains, tests, matrices, benchmarks, and accepted residual risks.
tasks.description:
Release 1.0 only after correctness, UB, security, ABI, performance, docs, examples, and support gates are satisfied.

PRD: §25 M10, §26
